title: horkelia clevelandii var. brevibracteata
intro: Horkelia clevelandii var. brevibracteata, also known as Cleveland's horkelia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the saxifrage family, native to the Pacific coast of North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Cleveland's horkelia include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Horkelia clevelandii var. brevibracteata are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, which can be used topically or internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
